lede: A look at June 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Diego Velázquez and Pierre Corneille, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Syrian civil war
events.desc: Syrian civil war: The Battle of Raqqa begins with an offensive by the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) to capture the city from the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL).

events.title: Hughes Airwest Flight 706
events.desc: Hughes Airwest Flight 706 collides with a McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II of the United States Marine Corps over the San Gabriel Mountains, killing 50.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The United States Navy's victory over the Imperial Japanese Navy at the Battle of Midway is a major turning point in the Pacific Theater. All four Japanese fleet carriers taking part—Akagi, Kaga, Sōryū and Hiryū—are sunk, as is the heavy cruiser Mikuma. The American carrier Yorktown and the destroyer Hammann are also sunk.

events.title: Novarupta
events.desc: The eruption of Novarupta in Alaska begins. It is the largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century.

events.title: Shewa
events.desc: The Shewan forces of Menelik II of Ethiopia defeat the Gojjame army in the Battle of Embabo. The Shewans capture Negus Tekle Haymanot of Gojjam, and their victory leads to a Shewan hegemony over the territories south of the Abay River.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The First Battle of Memphis, a naval engagement fought on the Mississippi River, results in the capture of Memphis, Tennessee by Union forces from the Confederates.

events.title: Alexis St. Martin
events.desc: Alexis St Martin is accidentally shot in the stomach, leading to William Beaumont's studies on digestion.

events.title: War of 1812
events.desc: War of 1812: In the Battle of Stoney Creek, considered a critical turning point in the war, a British force of 700 under John Vincent defeats an American force twice its size under William Winder and John Chandler.

events.title: Shivaji
events.desc: Shivaji is crowned as the first Chhatrapati of the Maratha Empire at Raigad Fort.

events.title: War of the League of Cambrai
events.desc: War of the League of Cambrai: In the Battle of Novara, Swiss troops defeat the French under Louis II de la Trémoille, forcing them to abandon Milan; Duke Massimiliano Sforza is restored.

births.name: Diego Velázquez
births.line: (date of baptism), Spanish painter and educator

births.name: Italo Balbo
births.line: Italian air marshal and fascist politician who played a key role in developing Mussolini's air force

births.name: Albert II of Belgium
births.line: King of the Belgians from 9 August 1993 to 21 July 2013 (abdicated)

births.name: Thomas Mann
births.line: German author and critic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Tommie Smith
births.line: American sprinter and football player; winner of 1968 Olympic 200m gold medal in a world record time

deaths.text: Stan Getz, American saxophonist and jazz innovator
Robert F. Kennedy, American soldier, lawyer, and politician, 64th United States Attorney General
Lumière brothers, French film director, producer, and screenwriter
Gerhart Hauptmann, German novelist, poet, and playwright, Nobel Prize laureate
William II, Count of Flanders, Count of Flanders

observances.text: Christian feast day: Ini Kopuria (Anglican Church of Melanesia, Church of England, Episcopal Church)
Christian feast day: Blessed Maria Laura Mainetti
